# Questions in Livestream 13

Live Stream #13: Triskaidekaphobia

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=208" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28</a> | Were the Libyan pharaohs distinct in some way, and what were their reigns like? | Falk described their era as a fragmented Third Intermediate Period in which Libyan rulers imitated Egyptian kingship while retaining some cultural distinctives of their own. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=412" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:52</a> | Where was Caphtor, and is it the same as Keftiu? | He said the majority view treats them as different, with the identification of Caphtor still debated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=487" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:07</a> | Has he seen the movie *Gods of Egypt*, and is it worse than *Exodus: Gods and Kings*? | He had not seen *Gods of Egypt*, but said *Exodus: Gods and Kings* was bad and that he would watch the other film to compare. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=535" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:55</a> | What books would he recommend for understanding Revelation through Old Testament symbolism? | He recommended Grant Osborne's Revelation commentary and pointed toward newer Old Testament-in-the-New-Testament style resources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=648" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:48</a> | Will he ever release a solo album? | Maybe someday. He said he does make music, but it remains a time-limited hobby. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=720" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:00</a> | If Egypt was weak under Siamun, how could he have campaigned in Palestine and burned Gezer? | Falk questioned parts of that reconstruction and said the issue needs more evidence; a weak king could still mount a limited campaign, but he was not persuaded by the common framing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=912" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:12</a> | How can Genesis be intertextual with Egyptian and Mesopotamian myths if they were written in different languages? | He said multilingualism and translation were normal in the ancient Near East, so traditions could cross linguistic boundaries without difficulty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=1280" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:20</a> | What are the main outstanding problems or inconsistencies in our knowledge of Egypt or the ancient Near East? | He pointed especially to unresolved chronological details and finer political questions, while saying the larger historical framework is usually more stable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=1629" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:09</a> | Is the suffering servant connected with Job, and when was Job written? | He said the comparison works at the level of archetype, and he would not date Job earlier than the early Iron Age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=1812" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:12</a> | Besides Labib Habachi and Zahi Hawass, does he know other Egyptologists from Egypt? | He said yes, though he did not give a developed list on stream. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=2014" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:34</a> | Are there first-hand Egyptian accounts of people claiming to witness magic the way the Bible reports miracles? | He said not really; Egyptian texts usually assume magic is real rather than dwelling on eyewitness-style validation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=2344" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:04</a> | What books would he recommend for Egyptian law, magic, and wisdom, especially for Scripture context? | He recommended starting broadly with *The Context of Scripture* and pointed to specialist works such as Ritner on Egyptian magic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=2779" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:19</a> | If there is no direct connection between Satan and Baal, what should we make of Jesus linking Beelzebub and Satan? | He said the connection is indirect: later Jewish demonology could place Beelzebub under Satan, but that is not the same as claiming Baal and Satan were originally the same being. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=3301" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:01</a> | Has he thought about putting Sothic chronology into the Groundhog Lab? | He said no, because Sothic chronology is debated and not something he would treat as a simple settled input. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=3744" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:24</a> | Could "Yahud Melek" on Sheshonq's Bubastite Portal be Judah? | He said it could be related to Judah, but the word order is wrong for "king of Judah"; the damaged inscription may refer instead to a city or place connected with Judah. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=4062" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:42</a> | Does Genesis 1-5 show more Egyptian influence or more Mesopotamian influence? | He said both influences are present, and it is exceptionally hard to prove that one dominates the other. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=4218" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:18</a> | What is his opinion on reports of tobacco and cocaine traces in Egyptian mummies? | He treated those results as likely modern contamination from sloppy archaeology rather than evidence of ancient Egyptian use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=4567" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:07</a> | Who was Zerah the Cushite? | Falk thought the figure probably belongs in Egypt's 22nd Dynasty and may correspond to one of the Osorkons, but he did not want to be firmer without more research. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=4956" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:36</a> | What are good undergraduate schools for budding scholars interested in this field? | He said it depends on the student's goals, noted Chicago as strong for Assyriology, and said Wheaton's archaeology strength had already declined because the program was closing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=5196" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:36</a> | What should we make of claims that Serapis proves Jesus was made up? | He rejected the comparison as mythicist fringe argumentation and said Jesus' historical existence is overwhelmingly accepted by historians. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=5785" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:25</a> | How is his new plagues book going? | He said he had not had much chance to write because he still needed enough funding to take a sabbatical for the project. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=5856" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:36</a> | Would Genesis 1-5 have to be written during the exile to have Mesopotamian influence, or could it reflect Amorite influence in Avaris? | He said no exile date is required; stories like Gilgamesh circulated much earlier, and Avaris or Hyksos-Babylonian contact could have been one possible route of cultural interchange. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=5995" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:55</a> | Can someone fully learn Egyptian from grammars like Gardiner if they are not studying it at university? | He said grammars can take you a long way, but most people still need at least some help with the alphabet, phonetics, and getting started. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=6151" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:31</a> | Could the name Imhotep be equivalent to Joseph? | No. He said the names are neither historically nor etymologically related. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=6235" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:55</a> | What languages does he know, and does he know Arabic? | He said he is not conversant in Arabic, though he picks up some when traveling in Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=6479" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:59</a> | Did Psamtik I have his campaign stopped by the Scythians in the Levant? | He said he did not know enough about Scythian history to answer that confidently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=6721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:01</a> | What does he think about attempts to move Ahmose to 1570 BCE by redating Khyan? | He rejected that proposal, saying the wider Egyptian and Near Eastern chronology would not allow the shift. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=6877" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:37</a> | Can Akkadian be learned from textbooks alone? | In principle yes, but he said it is a brutal language to learn and is much better approached with instruction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=6997" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:37</a> | Is there any attestation supporting the idea that Yahweh was originally an Edomite or Midianite god? | He said a southern or Midianite connection is not absurd, but the evidence is limited and does not prove a simple origin story. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/orrXTt_zI6I?t=7288" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:28</a> | Does 1 Chronicles 7:20-24 show that Ephraim's tribe was already in the land before the Exodus? | He said no. He read the passage as clan language and genealogical compression, not as proof that the historical Ephraim was living in Canaan before the Exodus. |
